question.text: An investor submits a purchase application for a liquid fund at 2:00 PM on a business day with funds transferred via RTGS. Which NAV will be applicable?

answer.text: Same day's NAV since the application was received before 3 PM
Next business day's NAV since for liquid fund purchases, the cut-off time is 1:30 PM
Same day's NAV since RTGS provides immediate fund availability
Previous day's NAV as per liquid fund regulations

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For liquid fund purchases, the cut-off time is 1:30 PM, not 3 PM. Since the application was received at 2:00 PM (after 1:30 PM), the next business day's NAV will apply — even though the funds were available via RTGS and the time was before the general 3 PM cut-off.
**The Trap:** This is one of the most commonly confused cut-off scenarios in the exam. Students who remember the general 3 PM cut-off often forget the earlier 1:30 PM cut-off for liquid and overnight fund purchases. The mode of payment (RTGS) is irrelevant if the cut-off time is missed.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Different cut-off times exist for different fund categories and transaction types. For liquid fund REDEMPTIONS, the standard 3 PM cut-off applies. Only PURCHASES in liquid/overnight funds have the earlier 1:30 PM cut-off.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If a client wants to park money in a liquid fund for even one day of extra return, ensure the application is submitted before 1:30 PM. A common mistake is rushing to submit before 3 PM thinking it is the universal cut-off, only to miss the 1:30 PM deadline for liquid funds.
